# Abderrahim GHAZALI

> Software engineer since 2014. Builds automation systems, AI tooling, and web platforms. Based in Paris. Open to consulting and contract work.

## Pages

- [Home](https://abderrahimghazali.github.io/): Portfolio — selected work, side projects, stack, open source
- [About](https://abderrahimghazali.github.io/about/): Background, approach, and stack
- [Blog](https://abderrahimghazali.github.io/blog/): Articles on AI, automation, Drupal, and software engineering

## Blog Posts

- [The evals-first loop: how I stop agents from regressing silently](https://abderrahimghazali.github.io/blog/the-evals-first-loop/): A practical loop for catching agent regressions before they reach production — build the eval set first, then iterate.
- [Drupal migrations, 12 years in: the rules I wish I had at the start](https://abderrahimghazali.github.io/blog/drupal-migrations-12-years-in/): Hard-won rules for migrating content into Drupal, from someone who's been doing it since 2014.
- [n8n for engineers: when a workflow graph beats a cron job](https://abderrahimghazali.github.io/blog/n8n-for-engineers/): When n8n makes sense, when it doesn't, and how to use it without hating it.
- [Shipping a menubar SaaS alone: lessons from MRRTracker's launch](https://abderrahimghazali.github.io/blog/shipping-a-menubar-saas-alone/): What I learned launching a macOS menubar app solo — the mistakes, the wins, and the parts nobody talks about.
- [A pragmatic RAG stack for teams that already have a database](https://abderrahimghazali.github.io/blog/a-pragmatic-rag-stack/): You probably don't need a dedicated vector database. A practical RAG architecture using pgvector and what you already have.
- [We All Use AI to Code Now. Almost Nobody Trusts It.](https://abderrahimghazali.github.io/blog/we-all-use-ai-to-code-now-almost-nobody-trusts-it/): 84% of developers use AI coding tools daily. Only 29% trust the output. What that gap means.
- [Claude Mythos: The AI Model Too Dangerous to Release](https://abderrahimghazali.github.io/blog/claude-mythos-the-ai-model-too-dangerous-to-release/): Anthropic built its most capable model ever — then decided not to release it publicly.

## Stack

- AI / Agents: LangChain, LangGraph, OpenAI, Anthropic, pgvector, LlamaIndex, Ollama, n8n
- Languages: PHP, TypeScript, Python, Rust, Swift, Go, SQL, Bash
- Frameworks: Drupal, Symfony, Laravel, React, Next.js, Node.js, FastAPI, Tauri
- Infra: Docker, AWS, Postgres, Redis, Varnish, Elasticsearch, Terraform, GitHub Actions
